claim: Risk of cardiovascular events can be cut by a third by using antihypertensive drug therapy among hemodialysis patients.
evidence_sentences: Among 1202 patients who we identified in 5 studies, the overall benefit of antihypertensive therapy compared with the control or placebo group had a combined hazard ratio for cardiovascular events of 0.69 (95% CI: 0.56 to 0.84) using a fixed-effects model and 0.62 (95% CI: 0.45 to 0.86) using a random-effects model.
